slides.title: Why crushed garlic is *good for your heart*
slides.sub: A compound called allicin forms only when garlic is broken. It may help protect your aging cardiovascular system.

slides.title: Allicin needs a crush
slides.body: Whole garlic holds its benefit in reserve. Crushing or chopping *releases allicin*, the compound behind most of its heart effects.

slides.title: It relaxes your vessels
slides.body: Allicin helps blood vessels widen and stay supple. That *can support healthy blood pressure* as arteries naturally stiffen with age.

slides.title: Friendlier cholesterol
slides.body: Regular garlic intake is linked to *modestly lower LDL*, the cholesterol that builds up in arteries over the years.

slides.title: Crush, then wait
slides.body: Crush the cloves and let them rest about ten minutes before cooking. The pause *lets allicin develop* before heat arrives.

slides.title: A clove most days
slides.body: One or two fresh cloves folded into real meals is enough. Consistency matters *more than any single dose*.

slides.title: Small habits, *measured daily*.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns the food, sleep, movement and steps that protect your heart into one daily Health Score you can actually follow.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Garlic only gives up its best trick once you crush it. That is when allicin forms. 🧄🌿

It can support healthy blood pressure, relax your vessels and is linked to lower LDL as you age. Crush, wait ten minutes, then cook. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns heart-friendly habits into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Crushed Garlic Is Good for Your Heart
